title: Lesson 2: New Identity in Jesus
description: The gospel does not just forgive you. It changes who you are. Jesus is the eternal Word and true Light who entered your darkness, took your sin, gave you His righteousness, and clothed you with Himself. In Him, your old self was crucified, the new has come, and you no longer live from your own record. You are found in Christ.

blocks.text: In the last lesson, you met the Father who runs toward you and loves you before you ever love Him back. Now the next question is simple: if the Father loves me like that, then who am I? This lesson turns to Jesus, the Son who takes what is yours, gives what is His, and makes you new.

blocks.alt: Jesus as the Light of the world entering the darkness

blocks.title: The Light Came Down
blocks.text:
John begins his Gospel by pulling the camera all the way back. Before Bethlehem, before creation, before time itself, Jesus already was. He is the Word who was with God and was God. In Him was life, and that life was the light of men.

Then the wonder of the gospel: the Light came into the world He made. Some rejected Him and stayed hidden in darkness. But others received Him, believed in His name, and were given the right to become children of God.

Jesus did not come to leave you hiding in shame. He came so your sin could be brought into the light, punished at the cross, and replaced with His righteousness. That is the Great Exchange.
2.1 - When you picture Jesus as the Light coming into your darkness, what feels most personal to you: being seen, being forgiven, or being made new?
The True Light

John does not begin his Gospel with Jesus as a good teacher or a helpful example. He begins before creation, before time, before anything existed except God Himself. Jesus is the eternal Word, the Son who has always been with God and has always been God.

In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. He was in the beginning with God. All things were made through him, and without him was not any thing made that was made. In him was life, and the life was the light of men. The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.

Jesus is the light that darkness cannot overcome. That means your new identity does not begin with your self-image, your effort, or your past. It begins with who Jesus is. He is the perfect Son, perfectly loved by the Father, perfectly righteous in every thought, desire, word, and action. And the miracle of the gospel is that He brings His light into your darkness.

John says that to all who receive Jesus and believe in His name, He gives the right to become children of God (John 1:12). Not the chance to earn it. Not a trial period. The right. Jesus brings you into His own relationship with the Father, so your identity is no longer built on your record. It is built on Him.
Crucified With Christ
The gospel does not just forgive your past. It joins you to Jesus. Paul says the old self, the you defined by sin, shame, striving, and self-rule, was put to death with Christ.

I have been crucified with Christ. It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me. And the life I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me.

Paul does not say, "I improved myself with Christ." He says, I have been crucified with Christ. The old identity went to the cross. The new identity is Christ living in you. This is why Christianity is not just behavior change. It is a new life joined to Jesus.
For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

That phrase matters: put on Christ. Remember the robe the father wrapped around his son at the end of the last lesson? That robe was not just welcome-home clothing. It was a picture of this. Jesus is the robe. When the Father brought you home, He did not only open the door. He clothed you with His own Son's righteousness. You do not stand before God in your best effort. You stand before Him wearing Christ.
The Great Exchange

Christians have often called this the Great Exchange. Your sin is counted to Jesus. His righteousness is counted to you. That word righteousness means right standing with God. It is not a feeling and not a mood. It is the settled status Jesus gives you by faith.
For our sake he made him to be sin who knew no sin, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God.

This is the robe of righteousness. Your sin does not get ignored. It gets placed on Jesus. His righteousness does not stay far away from you. It covers you. The Great Exchange means you are not trying to stitch together an identity from your own goodness. You receive the perfect standing of Christ.
Walk through the exchange below slowly. Watch what Jesus takes, and what He gives.
2.2 - Where are you still trying to stand before God in your own record instead of receiving the righteousness of Jesus?
Found in Him

Paul had plenty of religious achievements, but he did not want to stand before God wearing any of them. He wrote that his one desire was to be found in Christ, not having a righteousness of his own, but the righteousness that comes through faith in Jesus (Philippians 3:9). That is the heart of Christian identity: you are not building a record of your own. You are found in Him.
Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come.

In Christ, you are not your old self with cleaner habits. You are a new creation. The old has passed away. The new has come. You are justified, because your guilt has been answered by Jesus. You are adopted, because the Son brings you into the Father's family. You are alive, because Christ now lives in you.

This changes daily life at the deepest level. You stop obeying to earn your identity and start obeying from the identity Jesus gave you. You do not obey to be clothed in Christ. You obey because you are clothed in Christ. You do not fight sin to get a relationship with God. You fight sin because Jesus has already brought you into one.

That is the shift: not having a righteousness of my own, but being found in Him. When you fail, you do not run back to self-hatred or self-improvement as your savior. You run back to Jesus, where the Light still shines, the robe still covers, and Christ still lives in you.

2.3 - Say this line out loud and sit with it for a moment: You stand before God wearing Christ. If that were the first sentence you believed every morning, what would change about how you handle failure, fight sin, and walk into your week?
This Week's Practice

Each morning this week, before the day gets loud, say this line out loud and sit with it for one minute: You stand before God wearing Christ. Let that be the first sentence of your day. Then ask one honest question: where am I tempted to live today as if my own record is still my identity?
Also this week, read John 1:1-18 in one sitting. Watch who Jesus is before you think about who you are. He is the Word, the Life, and the Light. Your identity is secure because it is found in Him.
Lesson 2 Review

Jesus is the eternal Word and true Light. At the cross, He took your sin and gave you His righteousness. In Him, your old self has been crucified, you are clothed in Christ, and you are a new creation. You do not live from your own record anymore. You are found in Him.
Closing Reflections
What is one key thing you learned from this lesson?
What is one step you are going to take this week?
What is a prayer request for this week?
